hawkish_phrases: it was premature to draw firm conclusions
ECB's asset purchase programmes and lending operations were working as intended
members expressed caution about adopting an unduly gloomy interpretation of recent data
survey-based measures of longer-term inflation expectations remained well anchored
there appeared to be no indications of a risk of deflation
dovish_phrases: downside risks to the outlook for inflation were generally considered to have remained high
the anticipated timing of inflation normalising towards 2% was likely to be pushed back again
a case could be made for considering reinforcing the ECB's accommodative monetary policy stance already at the current meeting
the degree of monetary policy accommodation would need to be re-examined at the December monetary policy meeting
the Governing Council was willing and able to act, if warranted, by using all available tools within its mandate
summary: The Governing Council held all three policy rates unchanged (main refinancing at 0.05%, deposit facility at -0.20%) with no dissent disclosed, but adopted a clear easing bias by signaling a comprehensive review of policy options for December 2015 amid concerns about downside inflation risks and the protracted period of low inflation pushing back the return to target.
